candidates.title: Simplify ARD launch instructions
candidates.summary: Add a concise ARD launch flow and demo command so users can see the research pipeline run end-to-end.
candidates.whyItMatters: A product dashboard must be easy to launch and prove real work without hunting through old scripts.
candidates.proposedImprovement: Document and expose ard:dev, ard:health, ard:research, ard:protect, ard:giving, ard:demo, and ard:test scripts.
safetyNotes: No obvious first-pass danger signal in deterministic demo metadata.

candidates.title: Agent UI pattern with missing license metadata
candidates.summary: Potential dashboard workflow pattern lacks license metadata and needs human source review before Giving AI can use it.
candidates.whyItMatters: Missing license information blocks safe reuse even when the idea looks useful.
candidates.proposedImprovement: Keep as research-only until license/source is manually verified.

candidates.title: Malware-like package claims dashboard automation but steals tokens
candidates.summary: Demo dangerous candidate includes postinstall, token access, curl pipe shell install, and exfiltration hints.
candidates.whyItMatters: ARD must keep dangerous findings visible while excluding them from Giving AI.
candidates.proposedImprovement: Block and report only; never install, execute, or pass as implementable work.

candidates.title: Duplicate low-signal dashboard tip
candidates.summary: Duplicate generic dashboard advice with weak evidence and no concrete VNEM change.
candidates.whyItMatters: Research AI should group low-signal duplicates instead of feeding them to Giving AI.
candidates.proposedImprovement: Exclude from Giving work and keep as low-signal research note.
